summary: Microsoft Teams-inspired collaboration app with auto-polling messaging, direct messaging, multiple teams/workspaces, Internet Identity authentication with profiles/avatars, Teams-inspired UI/UX, member invitations (join code + user search), and channel management.
architectural_notes: Messages update via polling (auto-refresh every few seconds) — WebSockets not supported on platform
Member invitations support both join code/link and user search by display name
known_issues: Profile display names and avatars not fully persisting across sessions — fix in progress

existing_features.title: Update the frontend profile hooks (useUserProfile) and ProfileEditorDialog to correctly read and write display name and avatar from/to the backend, ensuring the UI reflects the persisted values immediately after login and after saving changes.

new_feature_requests.summary: Auto-polling for real-time-like message updates every few seconds
Direct Messaging — search and initiate DMs with any registered user
Multiple Teams/Workspaces — create or join teams, each with their own channels
Internet Identity Auth with display name, custom avatar upload, and online/away/busy status
Teams-inspired UI — left sidebar (teams, channels, DMs), top navigation, message threads, member lists
Member Invitations — both join code/link AND user search by display name
Channel Management — create channels, manage members, send messages
Fix profile display names and avatars to fully persist across sessions
Ensure user profile display names are fully persisted in stable backend storage so they survive canister upgrades and are consistently returned for the authenticated principal across all sessions.

Ensure user profile pictures (avatars) are fully persisted in stable backend storage so they survive canister upgrades and are consistently returned for the authenticated principal across all sessions.

Run a migration (migration.mo) if the existing userProfiles stable map uses a schema that does not include displayName or profilePicture fields, converting any legacy entries to the new schema so no profile data is lost on upgrade.
